heading: COLLEGE CREDIT PROGRAM
operative_text: Each school district shall implement a program under which students may earn the equivalent of at least 12 semester credit hours of college credit in high school. On request, a public institution of higher education in this state shall assist a school district in developing and implementing the program.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt: a secondary education student who has entered the first course in a sequence of two or more technical courses for three or more credits in a career and technical education program; or a student who: is enrolled in an academic or workforce course that is part of a sequence of courses leading to an industry-recognized credential, certificate, or degree; and has declared that sequence of courses as the student's major course of study.
career and technical education courses approved by the State Board of Education or innovative courses approved by the State Board of Education that are provided for local credit.

_note: Subsection (b) appears in two amended versions (H.B. 505 and H.B. 18, both 84th Leg.); both included verbatim as in source. No truncation.
